practices.name: teaching
practices.description: Lessons, marking, classroom behaviour, reports, department admin.

practices.name: choir
practices.description: Community choir — Tuesday rehearsals, learning parts, the spring concert.

obs.body: Year 9 first thing, double lesson on rates of reaction. They were sharp this morning, good answers off Daniel and the back row. Nice start to the week.
Staffroom out of the good teabags again so it's the supermarket own-brand. Tragedy.
Year 11 period 2, mock feedback. Calm, they actually wanted to know where they dropped marks. Felt useful.
P5 with 8C and I snapped at Kayden for flicking a pipette lid. Whole class went silly after. Kept four back. Ended the day with a headache.
Choir tonight. Learning the Fauré for the spring concert, altos have a horrible jump in the middle section but we got there. Voice felt strong by the end.
Year 7 settled in nicely for the bunsen burner safety lesson. They love the licence test. No dramas.
Quick chat with Gerald in the corridor about the data drop deadline. Fine, just a reminder.
Lovely Year 10 group period 2, did flame tests, the room smelled of lithium for an hour. They were engaged the whole way.
Bus home was 25 minutes late. Stood in the cold reading the same advert for a driving school four hundred times.
Last lesson Friday, 9F. Lost the room completely over a paper aeroplane. Raised my voice more than I meant to. Glad it's the weekend.
Farmers market, got the good eggs and a sourdough. Sat in the square with a coffee. Needed a slow morning.
Monday, Year 8 first period. Particle model recap, they were brilliant, hands up everywhere. Good omen for the week maybe.
Year 11 period 2 again, electrolysis. Steady, focused. I like that class.
P5 7B and they would NOT stop talking over me. Confiscated three phones. Felt like I was shouting into the wind by 3pm.
Choir. Sectional for the altos tonight, just us in the small hall. Pitched the descant clean first try. Came home buzzing.
Year 9 morning, exothermic reactions practical. They handled the equipment carefully, I barely had to intervene. Patient sort of day.
Tea in the staffroom, someone brought in flapjack. The good kind with the chewy edges.
Year 10 second period, balancing equations. Worked the room, kept my cool even when half of them hadn't done the homework. Fine actually.
Fire drill ate the start of P5. By the time we got back in there was 20 minutes left and no point starting the titration. Wrote it off.
Last period 8C, the boiler's out so the lab was freezing, everyone in coats and nobody concentrating. Couldn't blame them really. Long afternoon.
Sunday. Repotted the basil that's been dying on the windowsill. Low expectations.
Reports for Year 9 due end of next week. Forty of them. Started the spreadsheet, comment bank is a graveyard.
Year 7 first thing, separating mixtures. Filtration paper everywhere but they were happy and so was I. Good lesson.
Sandwich at my desk again, skipped the staffroom. Not enough hours in the day.
P5 9F, snapped at them over a thrown eraser and kept the whole row behind. Bit much in hindsight. Came home with a headache.
Skipped choir tonight, too much on. Sent my apologies to the section lead. Sat at the kitchen table till ten.
P5 with 7B, my patience was paper-thin, told the whole class off for something two of them did. Felt unfair after but couldn't climb back down. Throat scratchy by home time.
Lunch at the desk again, no staffroom. The standard sentence about 'making valuable contributions to class discussion' is starting to taste like ash.
Last lesson 8C, voice going hoarse and I had no fight in me, just let them coast on a worksheet. Not my proudest. Came home and didn't speak to anyone.
Reports submitted, finally. Gerald replied 'thanks, we'll review' in his usual two words. Glass of wine and a bath.
Slept in, did nothing, watched three episodes of something forgettable. Voice still a bit rough. Good.
Back to it. Year 11 mocks results came in, period 2, we went through grade boundaries. They were mature about it. Nice morning.
Choir back on. Concert's six weeks out now, conductor's getting twitchy. We ran the Fauré start to finish, altos held the line. Voice felt good and open.
Year 8 morning, did a fun practical, elephant's toothpaste. The gasps. Marked their write-ups at lunch and actually enjoyed it. Stayed level all day.
Year 10 first period, moles calculations. Tough topic but I was patient, broke it down three different ways and most of them got there.
Rumour going round the staffroom that Ofsted are due 'any week now'. Everyone a bit jumpy. Probably nothing.
P5 9F and they tested me but I held it, made a joke instead of biting. They actually laughed and settled.
Colleague in the science office, Marian, it's her birthday, we did cake in the prep room. Felt nice and human.
Saturday. Long walk by the canal, no headphones, just the ducks. The Ofsted thing seems to have gone quiet, nobody's mentioned it in days.
Year 9 first period, acids and alkalis, universal indicator rainbow. They were genuinely delighted. Calm, good morning.
Choir, sat next to the new alto, she reads music well. We nailed the tricky entry in the Stanford. Sang for two hours and could've kept going.
Year 7 period 2, states of matter, did the modelling with marbles in a tray. They were lovely. I had all the patience in the world today.
Last lesson 7B, normally my nemesis, but I kept it light and they came with me. Surprised myself. Voice held up fine too.
Woke up streaming, properly full head cold, sinuses like concrete. Dosed up on paracetamol and dragged myself in.
Croaking through P5 with the cold, lost my voice by the last twenty minutes, had to write instructions on the board. Year 8 were quite sweet about it actually.
Voice completely gone today, full laryngitis from this virus. Got cover for P5. Home on the sofa with lemon and honey. Miserable.
Still snotty but the voice is creeping back. Cancelled choir, obviously, can't sing through a head cold. Resting it.
Cold finally lifting. Did the big shop, bought far too many oranges out of some vitamin-C guilt.
Year 11 first period, revision on organic chemistry, they're getting nervous about the real exams. Steady morning, kept them grounded.
Year 10 reports due Friday. Comment bank reopened, soul leaving body.
Worked through lunch at my desk, no staffroom today.
P5 8C and I lost it over a thrown rubber, sent two out. Sat in the empty lab after feeling like the worst version of myself. Throat tight by the end.
Skipped choir again, couldn't face it. Hate missing it twice in a term. Sat up late at the kitchen table.
Last lesson 9F, frayed, snapped at the wrong kid entirely, had to apologise. Voice scratchy and thin again. Long week.
Reports in. Gerald stopped by the lab beforehand to 'check progress', stood over my shoulder a minute. Submitted at 4. Knackered.
Sunday market, ran into a parent which is always slightly weird, but they were nice about it. Bought tulips.
Year 9 first period, rates revision, they were on it, racing each other through the questions. Lovely energy. Good Monday.
Made it to choir. Concert's a fortnight off now. We did a full run and the altos sounded rich, my voice opened right up by the second half. Felt like myself again.
Year 8 period 2, combustion practical, they were careful and curious. I had a long fuse all lesson. Good morning.
P5 7B and somehow I kept the lid on, redirected the loud ones without raising my voice. They responded. Decent afternoon.
Wet break duty, herding damp Year 7s out of the corridors. Smelled of wet coat and orange squash. Standard.
Last lesson 8C and I stayed patient even when the bunsen got 'accidentally' left roaring. Calm word, not a blow-up. Better day all round.
Bought a new black dress for the concert. The old one's gone bobbly. Feeling oddly ready for it.
Year 10 first period, going gently into electrolysis again, they remembered more than they thought. Patient, easy morning.
Final rehearsal before the concert. Conductor near tears. The Fauré landed, altos and all. Voice clear right to the top of the range. Ready.
Concert done. We sang the Stanford and the Fauré and people actually cried. My voice held the whole way through. Floated home.
